generations.notes: Baseline FSM vs FSM. P1 dominates (93% P1+TO rate). Code state includes directional defense_pos fix and multi-faction FSM.

scripts: various combat micro, economy, production scripts
generations.notes: Gens 1-9 all failed. Combat scripts override FSM (catastrophic). Economy scripts steal building budget. Production scripts with >= 100 food thresholds never trigger (food peaks at ~95 during idle windows).
First script to trigger (80 food threshold). Net neutral.
BREAKTHROUGH. Smart production fill. 7% -> 53% P0 win rate. Now obsolete — FSM does own production fill.
FSM SYMMETRY FIX v1: directional defense_pos, map-center fallbacks, mirrored forward_pos, pre-seeded enemy_spawn.
Abilities breakthrough (LoafMode/Zoomies/DissonantScreech). P0 effectively dominates 8/10 seeds.
NEW BEST (old code). Pure combat micro: group focus fire + retreat wounded + push when clear. 0% -> 50% P0 win rate.
CATASTROPHIC. Ability commands interfere with focus fire. DO NOT combine ability and combat scripts.
CATASTROPHIC. Per-unit focus scatters damage. Group centroid focus is always better.

Worker spawn offset mirrored toward map center (was always +x, putting P0 workers closer to resources). Reduced P0 advantage from 90% to 80%. Remaining asymmetry is map-gen level (even-sized grid off-by-one in rotational symmetry).
Post-worker-spawn-fix FSM-only baseline (20 seeds). P0 still wins 80% due to map-gen asymmetry. P0 attacks first on most seeds.
Gen 21 combat_micro given to P1 (disadvantaged side). P1 goes from 15% to 45% effective. Scripts genuinely help the underdog.
Kiting for ranged units + HQ targeting + building rally point + 30% retreat threshold. Kiting preserves army but causes many timeouts.
REGRESSION. Removed kiting, added aggressive push. Without kiting, P0 kills ranged units and wins more. Kiting is essential.
Conditional kite (only when outnumbered) + aggressive push (when >=3 advantage). Best balance of preservation and aggression.
REGRESSION. Kite only at <20% HP. Less kiting = P0 converts more timeouts to wins. Broader kiting is better.
Slight regression from Gen 26. Base defense recall pulls units out of combat, losing advantage on some seeds.
CATASTROPHIC. Worker harassment splits army — one Nuisance detached weakens main force fatally. Never split army for harassment.
Shared Gen 26 scripts (both players). P0 effective 90%. Combat micro amplifies positional advantage when both sides have it. Scripts benefit the underdog most.
MAJOR IMPROVEMENT. Production building targeting (barracks/tech first, then HQ). Converts P1-leading timeouts to decisive wins by starving enemy reinforcements. 8→14 decisive P1 wins.
Gen 30 + commit mode (>=5 advantage, no retreat). Identical results — commit mode never triggers because armies rarely differ by 5+.
Push at ANY army advantage instead of >=3. Slightly worse — pushing into marginal fights loses some seeds.
Tighter retreat threshold (20% HP vs 30%). Same decisive wins, some are faster (~200 ticks). Marginal improvement.

NEW BEST. Closest-enemy focus fire (instead of weakest). Kills immediate threats faster, prevents army from chasing distant low-HP units. 14→16 decisive P1 wins. Only seeds 9,11 (P0 wins) and 2,4 (timeouts) remain.
Time-based aggression (push harder after tick 3000/4500). Late-game commit didn't convert timeouts — disabling kiting/retreat too early loses units.
Wider kite range (3 tiles instead of 2.2). Neutral — same results as Gen 30. Extra kite distance doesn't help.
Push at >=2 advantage (vs >=3). Same decisive wins but fewer P0 wins (1 vs 2). Middle ground between >=3 and any advantage.
Gen 34 closest-focus + late-game aggro. Same 16 P1 wins but 3 P0 wins (1 more than Gen 34). Late aggro converts a timeout but also loses a game.
Gen 34 + push >=2. One fewer win (seed 19 becomes timeout). Lower push threshold pushes into marginal fights.
Gen 34 + 20% retreat. Identical to Gen 34 results. Tighter retreat is neutral with closest-focus.
Gen 42 + push at +1 advantage. Worse than Gen 34 (15 vs 16 wins). Aggressive push into marginal fights loses seeds.
Per-unit threat kiting. 17 decisive wins, 0 timeouts (converted seeds 2,4,9). But 3 P0 wins (lost seed 15). Net: more decisive but lower effective dominance (85% vs 90%).
CATASTROPHIC. Hybrid weakest-near targeting confirmed terrible. Chasing wounded stalls army. Closest-to-centroid targeting is CRITICAL.
Gen 34 + formation_orient as shared script. Neutral — formation as separate script has no measurable impact.
Gen 34 + inline formation (tanks forward, ranged back). 17 wins, 0 timeouts. Converted seeds 2,3,4 from timeouts. Better than separate formation script.
CATASTROPHIC. DPS-weighted targeting (damage/hp ratio). 50/50 result — targeting high-DPS units instead of closest scatters damage and loses focus fire advantage.
Melee focus closest, ranged focus weakest. Worse — splitting targets between roles breaks unified focus fire.
CATASTROPHIC. Kill-secure targeting (lowest absolute HP). 50/50 — targeting wounded enemies anywhere in range causes army to chase, same failure mode as weakest targeting.
Threat-count targeting (most-threatening enemy). Slightly worse — high-threat enemy often equals closest anyway, extra budget cost of threats_to not worth it.
Centroid-bias kiting (70% flee + 30% toward army center). Worse — blending flee direction reduces kite effectiveness, more timeouts.
HP-threshold kite (ranged kite when own HP < 70%). 17 wins, only 1 P0 win. Good individual improvement — protects injured ranged units.

MAJOR IMPROVEMENT. Timed push at tick 4000 (force aggression in final third). 18 decisive P1 wins, ZERO P0 wins. Disables retreat and kiting in late game, forces all-in push. Only seeds 11,14 timeout.
Staged push (melee vanguard, ranged wait for engagement). Same decisive wins as Gen 34 but 0 P0 wins. More timeouts — ranged waiting delays pushes.
Gen 060 + Gen 059 (timed push + HP kite). REGRESSION — HP kite conflicts with timed push. Kiting pulls ranged units back when they should commit in late game.

NEW ALL-TIME BEST. Gen 060 + Gen 053 (timed push + inline formation). 19/20 P1 wins, 0 timeouts, only seed 8 lost. Formation gives better pre-combat positioning, timed push converts all close games. Won seeds 9,11 (previously structural P0 wins).
Gen 060 + Gen 059 + Gen 053 (all three). REGRESSION — HP kite again conflicts with timed push, causing 5 timeouts. Less is more: formation + timed push without extra kiting is optimal.

key_insights: CLOSEST-ENEMY focus fire (target nearest to centroid, not weakest) is the best targeting — kills immediate threats, prevents chasing distant low-HP units.
Production building targeting (barracks/tech first) during push is a major improvement — starves enemy reinforcements.
GROUP focus fire (centroid-based, all units target same enemy) is the single most impactful script behavior.
Retreat wounded units (<30% HP) when outnumbered preserves army for attrition advantage.
Push to enemy buildings when clear or >=3 advantage converts timeouts to wins.
Conditional kite (only when outnumbered) for ranged units is essential — preserves army without causing stalemates.
Push threshold sweet spot is >=3. Lower (>=2 or any) pushes into marginal fights. Higher never triggers.
Timed push at tick 4000 is the BIGGEST single improvement since closest-focus (16→18 P1 wins). Disabling retreat and kiting in late game forces decisive outcomes.
Inline formation (tanks forward, ranged back for idle units) converts close games. Works because it doesn't interrupt combat, only repositions idle units.
Formation + timed push is the BEST combination (19/20 P1 wins). Formation gives better pre-combat positioning, timed push forces decisive outcomes.
HP-threshold kiting (ranged kite when own HP < 70%) is good solo but CONFLICTS with timed push — kiting in late game prevents the all-in commit.
DPS-weighted targeting (damage/hp ratio) and kill-secure targeting (lowest absolute HP) are both CATASTROPHIC (50/50). Any non-closest targeting breaks focus fire.
Split-role targeting (melee closest, ranged weakest) breaks unified focus fire. All units must target the same enemy.
Centroid-bias kiting (blending flee direction toward army center) reduces kite effectiveness and adds timeouts.
Separate formation script (formation_orient as shared) has no impact. Inline formation integrated into combat_micro is effective because it avoids command conflicts.
Wider kite range (3 tiles vs 2.2) is neutral. Default sqrt(5) kite distance is fine.
Tighter retreat (20% vs 30%) is neutral. No meaningful difference.
smart_fill is counterproductive on current FSM (FSM does own production fill).
Ability commands interfere with attack commands — DO NOT combine ability and combat scripts.
Per-unit focus fire scatters damage. Group focus fire concentrates it. Always prefer group.
Never split army for worker harassment — concentrated force always wins.
Base defense recall hurts — units stop fighting and lose combat advantage.
Scripts benefit the UNDERDOG most: P1 goes from 15% to 95% with combat micro (Gen 63).
Shared scripts amplify positional advantage — the stronger side benefits more from symmetric scripts.
Worker spawn offset was a major asymmetry source (fixed). Remaining asymmetry is map-gen level.
Map has ~80% P0 baseline advantage after all fixes — caused by even-grid rotational symmetry off-by-one.
Previously 'structural' P0 seeds (9, 11) were conquered by Gen 63. Only seed 8 remains as P0 win. Timeouts eliminated.
Less is more in script combinations — adding HP kite to timed push hurts. The best combo is exactly 2 features: formation + timed push.
